fact_claims.claim: As of 2026-04-22, coding-session recall is not yet first-class in the current OpenClaw memory setup.

evidence_refs.note: Daily memory note explicitly identifies coding-session recall as not yet first-class.
Wiki concept page captures intended practice and importance of coding recall.

fact_claims.claim: The main practical memory gaps identified on 2026-04-22 were lack of automatic coding-task recall preflight, weak project and task episodic summaries, and under-populated wiki artifacts despite bridge mode being enabled.

evidence_refs.note: Daily memory note lists the practical gaps directly.

reflections.observation: Nightly reconciliation still depends mostly on daily memory files and local wiki pages because bridge mode is enabled but exported public memory artifacts remain unavailable.
reflections.pattern: Configured bridge reconciliation has stayed ahead of actual bridge artifact export for several nightly runs in a row.
reflections.recommended_action: Keep treating bridge-export absence as an operational gap, continue evidence-first ingestion from daily notes and wiki pages, and avoid overstating compiled-wiki coverage until exports appear.

evidence_refs.note: Current wiki status still warns that bridge mode has zero exported artifacts.
Open questions report shows the bridge-export question persisted across multiple nightly syntheses.
